title: Peltaea acutifolia
taxonomy: Peltaea acutifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Peltaea, which is a group of plants commonly known as barberry trees or acacia. The Malpighiaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Peltaea acutifolia is likely native to South America, where many other species of the Malpighiaceae family are found. The classification of Peltaea acutifolia is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf and flower structure.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help in treating various health issues.  The plant is used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
